$comment: ClawSecure-style runtime config for muhaven-rwa-skill. Loaded by the NemoClaw sandbox at boot and supplements the manifest's permissions block. Values here are NOT user-configurable — they ship with the signed skill bundle and any change forces a re-publish + re-sign + re-scan.

network.$comment_egress_advisory_in_host_native: Mirrors manifest.json#permissions.network — prod hosts only. Staging rehearsals (§3 Path (a) in AGENTIC_TEST_PLAN.md) install the same signed artifact with `user_config.backend_url=https://api-stage.muhaven.app` and rely on plain OpenClaw `sandbox.fallback: host_native` (advisory, not kernel-enforced). A future runtime that enforces this allowlist would block the staging override; that is intentional — the published artifact is prod-pure.

egress_allowlist.purpose: MuHaven backend — REST API + agent policy + intent confirmation
MuHaven dashboard origin — deep-link target for >$5K confirmations and device-code authorization (/link)

unix_socket.$comment_path_pattern_alignment: Path pattern mirrors `defaultBrokerEndpoint()` in `@muhaven/mcp/src/config.ts` (POSIX: `${HOME}/.muhaven/broker.sock`; Windows: named pipe). The pre-2026-05-12 pattern `${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR:-/run/user/${UID}}/muhaven-broker.sock` failed silently on macOS (no `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` default + no `/run/user/${UID}`) and Windows (no `$UID`), so a kernel-enforced runtime would have refused the broker IPC on those platforms. Today this field is host_native-advisory, but it now aligns with the broker code so a future kernel-enforced runtime passes through.

post_install_review.$comment: ClawHub displays this checklist on first install — investor must explicitly tick each item before the skill becomes active. Mirrors OpenClaw's user-consent screen for sensitive permissions.

items.title: muhaven-broker daemon must be running and authenticated.

muhaven-broker binary reachable on $PATH. ClawHub install only resolves the skill bin into a runtime-local node_modules; the broker (shipped in @muhaven/mcp) may not be on $PATH unless installed separately via `npm install -g @muhaven/mcp`.
This skill never moves funds without your confirmation. Three-tier dispatch is enforced server-side.
Network egress is allowlisted to MuHaven origins only. Any other endpoint is blocked at the runtime.
JWT lives in your OS keychain via the broker. The skill cannot prompt you to paste a token.

$comment_unknown_field_handling: Per OpenClaw issue #28298: unknown fields in this document MUST be treated as deny by the runtime. Never as allow. Forward-compat upgrades that introduce new permission types are gated by manifest.compatibility.openclaw_runtime.min.
